ref: Abram bowed with his face to the ground, and God said:
text: Abram gba, ka zulisi o zu tiŋŋa, Naawunni yeli u.
translated: Abraham took and followed him to the town, Naawunni said.

ref: Once, when the flocks were mating, I dreamed that all the rams were either spotted or speckled.
text: “Buŋkobiri maa duubu jemani, m daa zaasini zaasim, a nye maa, gbeesi dima la daa n bani chericheri, ka seesi gba mari booma.
translated: Buŋkobiri followed me to Jemani, and he did the Zaasini Zaasim work, and she is mine; the Gbeesi Dima there did something with Bani Chericheri, and then he saw the water was good.

ref: They took sheep, goats, donkeys, and everything else that was in the town or the countryside.
text: Ba gbaa ba peeri, buusi, naagi, a ni buŋsi ni bunso zaa n ba tiŋŋa maa puwa, a ni ba puuri niŋi.
translated: He works for money, buys food and water, and when he goes to the market in the morning, he returns home in the evening.

ref: When I send clouds over the earth, and a rainbow appears in the sky,
text: Jemanso zaa ka sagbana ti pili, sasuwa maa ni yi,
translated: The enemy has taken the child, we will come for you

ref: Rachel was buried beside the road to Ephrath, which is also called Bethlehem.
text: A zu, Rachel daa n kpini yi Efirata soori, ba pi u sogbarimini. .
translated: And then, Rachel died in Ephratah, and there she was buried.
